mocks.name: MOCK-S2-001
mocks.reason: Stub enqueueLeave no-op closure in buildDispatcher; real MarmotContext closure provided in S4

implementation_summary: Created leaveHandler.ts following the pollHandler.ts factory pattern exactly — synchronous handle(), LeaveHandlerDeps with single enqueueLeave dep, createLeaveIntentHandler factory. Registered in registerHandlers.ts via Option A: enqueueLeave added to HandlerDeps (required type), stub fallback `deps.enqueueLeave ?? (() => {})` with MOCK-S2-001 comment. AR-1 and AR-3 still hold. 4 new unit tests cover happy path, two malformed-payload variants, and dispatcher LRU dedup.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: The leaveHandler handle() function is synchronous (returns void, not Promise<void>) because it does no async work — enqueueLeave is a plain callback. The test template from pollHandler.test.ts uses `await expect(handler.handle(...)).resolves.not.toThrow()`, which only works when handle() returns a Promise. Applying the same pattern to the synchronous handler caused two test failures on the first run. The fix was trivial (switch to `expect(() => handler.handle(...)).not.toThrow()`), but the template pattern does not document this contingency.
